The indexed records do not contain the carrier CDR files themselves - only the carrier confirmation and SBC log findings [Data: Sources (8)]. 7) Printing: missing printers, Access Denied, jobs vanish / stuck - Multiple documented patterns and fixes appear in the records: - GPO not applied / new user: gpupdate /force + re‑login successfully applied printer mappings in user sessions [Data: Sources (39)]. - Queue paused / jobs stuck: re‑activate server queue and restart jobs (worked in a recorded ticket) [Data: Sources (27)]. - Printer queue/share corruption after Windows update + VM restore (SAP print servers VRINFRGERPRNT03/04): spooler restart did not help, a VM restore was performed and then administrators cleared SAP backlog by disabling/re‑enabling the printer network share - that action cleared queued jobs and restored printing in that incident [Data: Sources (28, 29)]. - Access Denied was resolved in one case by removing an explicit Deny ACE on the queue [Data: Sources (3)]. - Basel printer jobs briefly appearing then vanishing were tied to reachability/routing/firewall between print server and the printer; a firewall/routing adjustment restored printing [Data: Sources (22)]. B - What the indexed records do NOT contain (limits / where live evidence is required) - The indexed records do not contain your tenant's live D365/AOS/IIS logs, Exchange Event Log exports for your hosts, SBC logs/pcaps for the weekend window, firewall/WAF change diffs from your devices, or the print‑server Event Log/spooler crash dumps for your environment. Those live artifacts are required to prove root cause and to take a targeted fix. The prior cases document the investigative artefacts that were used, but they do not contain your environment's current logs [Data: Sources (0, 8, 23, 28, 32)]. C - Step‑by‑step resolution plan (mixing documented prior‑case fixes where available and recommended actions where the records do not contain the live data) High‑level classification (documented precedent) - Based on the breadth of services affected (D365, Exchange/SMTP, Teams/Direct‑Routing, SBC/carrier handoff, printing + AD/GPO), prior similar cases were handled as second_level_support and required coordinated engineering teams: Identity/AD, Exchange/Mail, Unified Communications / SBC, Network/Firewall, Print Infrastructure and Application/D365 teams [Data: Sources (0, 8, 16, 23, 28)]. Recommendation (classification): classify this ticket as second_level_support (escalate immediately). This matches the precedent in the indexed incidents [Data: Sources (0); Sources (8); Sources (16)]. Step 1 - Open cross‑functional incident bridge & preserve evidence (recommended but matches prior practice) - Action (recommended): Immediately open a 2nd‑level incident bridge and notify Network/Firewall, UC/SBC, Mail/Exchange Hybrid, D365/AppOps, Print Infrastructure/Core Engineering, and Identity/AzureAD owners. The indexed cases show these domains were engaged in prior multi‑service incidents [Data: Sources (0, 8, 23, 28)]. - Evidence preservation (required): request and preserve the following for the weekend window (these are the exact artefacts used in prior incidents): - D365/App: AOS server logs, Report Designer error traces, Data Mart / FRDB export or reset logs, and the Microsoft support package if already opened [Data: Sources (0); Sources (11)]. - Exchange/Mail: on‑prem Exchange Event Log extracts (filter for "535 5.7.3 Authentication unsuccessful" and related SMTP auth events), Message Trace exports, hybrid connector logs, and Conditional Access audit events [Data: Sources (32)]. - Teams/UC: Teams Admin Center diagnostics, Get‑CsTenantFederationConfiguration output, Teams call records/call analytics (if available), SBC SIP traces (INVITE/200/404/BYE), and SBC CDRs - note that prior incidents used SBC logs and carrier confirmations as primary evidence [Data: Sources (26); Sources (8); Sources (16)]. - Network/Firewall/WAF: firewall/WAF change logs and configuration diffs for the weekend (device audit export), and firewall logs showing blocked RTP/UDP for Zurich case reference [Data: Sources (15); Sources (23)]. - Printing: PrintServer System/Application/PrintService event logs, spooler state, any kernel crash dumps (if BSODs), published SMB share enable/disable timestamps, and GPO application traces (gpupdate attempts). Previous print incidents explicitly asked for these [Data: Sources (28, 29, 39)]. Step 2 - Reproduce / validate quick fixes (documented, perform in controlled tests) - D365: attempt to reproduce the Report Designer failure in a controlled session and capture AOS/IIS error logs. Prior cases escalated when report metadata (authors = "unknown") and FRDB corruption were present; FRDB/Data Mart reset and Microsoft escalation were the recorded remediation path where in‑place recovery failed [Data: Sources (0); Sources (11)]. - If a vendor case does not already exist, open Microsoft support (the records cite a Microsoft case for a similar problem) [Data: Sources (0)]. - Exchange/Outlook send failures: confirm that the same message sends successfully from OWA/mobile (as you observed). Collect the on‑prem Exchange Event Log entries with the "535 5.7.3 Authentication unsuccessful" string if present - prior ticket used that exact Event Log string as the diagnostic trigger and then adjusted Conditional Access/SMTP AUTH settings [Data: Sources (32)]. - Teams presence: run Get‑CsTenantFederationConfiguration and review External Access/Presence settings. In the recorded case, adjusting AllowPublicUsers / EnablePresencePrivacyMode / Presence Sharing fixed external presence for partners [Data: Sources (26)]. - Zurich audio: run a controlled test call while temporarily opening the broader dynamic RTP port range (as a test) - prior Zurich case used temporary port relaxation to prove the firewall was blocking media; once validated, apply permanent firewall config aligned to the Direct‑Routing blueprint [Data: Sources (23); Reports (5)]. - Basel ~30s drops: capture SBC logs for failing calls (SIP traces). Prior incidents showed SBC BYE at ~29-31s and were remediated by aligning NAT/session timeouts and Session‑Expires to the Direct‑Routing blueprint [Data: Sources (16); Reports (10)]. - New hotline numbers: check SBC translation/normalization table for the new range; the indexed record shows a missing translation entry caused immediate failures and adding a temporary translation rule validated delivery, then a permanent update was applied [Data: Sources (8)]. If carrier correlation is needed, request carrier CDRs (records show carrier confirmed delivery but the carrier traces were not attached in the indexed tickets) [Data: Sources (8)]. Step 3 - Apply documented remediations where prior cases recorded success (do these only after testing) - Teams/Firewall (Zurich): if temporary RTP port opening restores media in tests, then align firewall rules to allow the dynamic RTP range used by Teams Media Bypass per the Direct‑Routing blueprint and monitor 48 h (the Zurich record did exactly this and validated stability after 48h) [Data: Sources (23); Reports (5)]. - SBC / new numbers: if SBC translation entry is missing, apply the temporary translation rule to validate calls, then make the permanent translation/routing table change and validate with test calls as recorded in TI‑1612036 [Data: Sources (8)]. - Basel ~30s drops: update NAT/session timeout and SBC session parameters to match blueprint (documented fix) and validate with test calls (prior case applied permanent timeout change after temporary test proved success) [Data: Sources (16)]. - Printing: when symptoms map to documented cases: - If queue is "Paused": reactivate queue and restart jobs (documented success) [Data: Sources (27)]. - If GPO mapping missing for a user: perform gpupdate /force and require a re‑login (documented successful step) [Data: Sources (39)]. - If SMB/share corruption suspected after a VM restore/Windows update (SAP case): disabling and re‑enabling the network share cleared the backlog in the prior incident; if BSODs followed a restore, preserve crash dumps and escalate to Core Engineering for forensic analysis / Windows rollback discussion [Data: Sources (28, 29)]. - If "Access Denied" for a single user: check for explicit Deny ACE and remove if found (documented fix) [Data: Sources (3)]. - If jobs appear then vanish and ping fails from server to printer: check network/firewall routing between print server and printer VLAN - firewall rule change restored reachability in the Basel case [Data: Sources (22)]. Step 4 - If fixes above do not restore service, escalate with preserved artifacts (documented escalation pattern) - D365: if in‑place remediations fail, escalate to Microsoft (the prior incident opened a Microsoft support case and performed FRDB/Data Mart actions) [Data: Sources (0); Sources (11)]. - Exchange: if Event Log 535 entries persist after Conditional Access adjustments, escalate to Mail/Infra engineering with exported Event Log lines, Message Trace and hybrid connector logs (the prior case used those artifacts to conclude tenant Security Defaults/CA changes were the cause) [Data: Sources (32)]. - UC/SBC: if media or call‑routing persists, escalate UC/Network with SBC SIP traces, SBC CDRs, Teams Admin Center call records and carrier CDRs - the prior SBC/number incidents coordinated carrier + SBC + UC engineering [Data: Sources (8); Sources (16); Sources (23)]. - Print: if VM restore caused BSODs or persistent corruption, escalate to Core Engineering / virtualization team with VM snapshot/restore context (INTER12203 / INTER12204 were recorded restore contexts in the SAP case), crash dumps and hypervisor logs (the SAP case explicitly requested rollback/forensic capture) [Data: Sources (28, 29)]. D - Immediate prioritized checklist you can execute now (practical, evidence‑based) (Each line shows which prior incident used the same step.) 1) Open 2nd‑level incident and invite: Network/Firewall (perimeter), Unified Communications / SBC, Mail (Exchange), D365/AppOps, Print Infrastructure / Core Engineering, Identity/Azure AD. Prior multi‑domain incidents used this cross‑team escalation pattern [Data: Sources (0); Sources (8); Sources (23); Sources (28)]. 2) Collect and attach these artefacts to the incident ticket (exact items cited in prior investigations): - D365: AOS/IIS error traces and Report Designer error dumps; Data Mart / FRDB logs; any vendor/Microsoft SR numbers [Data: Sources (0)]. - Exchange: Windows Event Log export around failure times (look for 535 5.7.3), Message Trace, hybrid connector logs and Conditional Access audit logs [Data: Sources (32)]. - Teams / UC: Teams Admin Center call diagnostics, Get‑CsTenantFederationConfiguration output, SBC SIP traces/CDR slices for failing call timestamps, and firewall/WAF diffs for the weekend window [Data: Sources (26); Sources (8); Sources (23)]. - Network/Firewall: firewall device config diff / audit export for the weekend (the Zurich WAF/firewall change was proven by device audit/inspection in the prior case) [Data: Sources (15); Sources (23)]. - Print: PrintService operational logs, spooler state, gpupdate logs, queue state, SMB share enable/disable timestamps, VM restore snapshot IDs and any crash dumps (SAP case used these artifacts) [Data: Sources (28, 29, 39)]. 3) Run the small, quick validation steps (documented fixes used successfully before): gpupdate /force and re‑login for affected users (GPO printer missing case) [Data: Sources (39)]; re‑activate paused queues and restart jobs (TI‑1621044) [Data: Sources (27)]; temporarily open RTP range for Zurich tests (documented successful test) [Data: Sources (23)]; apply temporary SBC translation rule to validate new hotline delivery (TI‑1612036) [Data: Sources (8)]. Do these only as controlled test steps and capture logs. 4) If you need carrier correlation for the hotline numbers, request carrier CDRs/SIP traces for the specific sample numbers and UTC windows - the indexed record shows carrier confirmation was used, but no carrier CDRs were attached in that case; asking the carrier for per‑call traces is the recommended next step when SBC logs need correlation [Data: Sources (8)]. E - Explicit statement of current limits and final recommendation (honest about what the indexed records cannot do) - The indexed knowledge set documents the kinds of root causes and the exact remediation steps that were effective in prior, similar incidents (examples and remediation recorded above). However, those records do NOT contain your site's live logs, firewall diffs, SBC traces/pcaps, Exchange Event Log exports or the print‑server crash dumps needed to prove the root cause for your environment. Therefore I cannot conclusively declare a single change as the universal root cause from the indexed corpus - the records show multiple distinct change events (firewall/WAF edits, NAT/session timeout adjustments, tenant policy changes, SBC translation omissions, Windows update + VM restore) that in prior tickets caused different subsets of your symptoms [Data: Sources (15, 23, 16, 8, 28)]. - Practical conclusion (evidence‑based): do NOT treat this as first‑line only. The correct classification is second_level_support and you should escalate immediately to the engineering owners named above. Prior incidents with the same symptom breadth were escalated and resolved by those teams [Data: Sources (0, 8, 16, 23, 28)]. If you want I will: - Draft the incident‑bridge invite and the exact artifact request text (one‑click copy/paste) for each owning team (Network, UC/SBC, Exchange, D365/AppOps, Print/Core Eng). This is a recommended next step (not verbatim in prior tickets) but it follows the exact evidence collection patterns used in the recorded incidents. - Or, if you prefer, confirm which of the listed logs you can gather now and I will annotate next actions for each log artifact (what to look for in the output, and which team to hand it to). Key references (examples from the incident corpus) - D365 / Report Designer & FRDB remediation and Microsoft escalation [Data: Sources (0); Sources (11)]. - Exchange SMTP AUTH / Event Log 535 and Conditional Access remediation [Data: Sources (32)]. - Teams presence / federation policy change and Get‑CsTenantFederationConfiguration checks [Data: Sources (26)]. - Zurich firewall RTP port blocking -> temporary open -> permanent fix per Direct‑Routing blueprint [Data: Sources (23)]. - Basel session timeout / SBC BYE at ~29-31s -> NAT/session timeout alignment [Data: Sources (16)]. - New hotline / SBC translation omission -> temporary translation rule -> permanent routing update; carrier confirmed delivery to SBC (but records do not contain carrier CDR attachments) [Data: Sources (8)]. - SAP printserver VM restore + spooler/network‑share corruption -> disabling/re‑enabling share cleared backlog; spooler restart alone did not fix; crash dumps and forensic capture were requested in that prior case [Data: Sources (28, 29)]. If you confirm you want the incident bridge opened now, give me (A) the primary incident contact(s) and (B) confirmation you authorize 2nd‑level escalation and I will produce the artifact request emails and the immediate triage sequence you should run.
